WTI.data_anomaly: fast_info prev_close $96.02 (-2.3%) vs history prev_close $93.50 (+0.3%). Using fast_info as headline; history used for technicals only.

macro_summary: Markets edged higher Tuesday with SPY gaining 0.3% to $670.79 and QQQ up 0.51% to $603.31, continuing the tactical relief rally that began Monday but still lacking breadth conviction — mega-cap tech remains below its 50-day averages while semis diverge higher. VIX fell a further 4.85% to 22.37, now down materially from last week's 27+ readings, though fear has compressed faster than fundamentals have improved. The FOMC two-day meeting began Tuesday with the rate decision due tomorrow (March 19); the Fed is widely expected to hold, and the market's focus will be on Chair Powell's language around the inflation trajectory and the pace of any future cuts. Micron reports earnings after the close today — a binary event for the memory chip and broader semis thesis given Micron's CEO's projection of roughly 40% memory price increases through Q2 2026 (a bullish call from a seller that needs independent corroboration).
commodities_note: WTI crude shows a data conflict: the pipeline's primary source reports $93.82 (-2.29% daily), while a secondary source flagged $96.02 as the prior close — the -2.29% daily figure is used as the headline but treat the exact level with some caution. The Hormuz crisis is now in its ninth day; per CNN, only 90 tankers have transited since March 1 versus a baseline of roughly 138 per day, yet Iran itself continues exporting oil through the strait, per reporting that cites Vortexa and Windward data. Gold dipped 0.52% to $5,004, pulling back from elevated levels but remaining well above structural support. The US dollar weakened 0.34% (DXY at 99.58), a mild tailwind for emerging markets and Singapore-listed names. Gold remains extended — no attractive entry exists at current levels despite the macro backdrop supporting precious metals.

D05_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. DBS is essentially hugging its 50-day average (SGD 57.54 vs. SGD 57.60 average) with RSI at 57.7 — technically neutral, no compelling entry dislocation exists for a cash investor. The Singapore banking thesis continues to face net interest margin compression from the Fed's rate-pause stance: as per SGX filings cited in the quarterly earnings context, all three major Singapore banks saw net interest income decline in Q4 2025, with the rate-pause narrative eliminating the carry-trade tailwind that drove DBS's outperformance in 2022-2023. At 13.9x forward earnings — in line with the Singapore cluster median — and a 5.36% dividend yield at 2.37x book, valuation is reasonable but not compelling enough to override the margin headwind. Revenue is declining 3.3% and analyst consensus forecasts an 8.8% earnings contraction. Upgrades to WATCH on a clean pullback to the SGD 57.00 support level with above-average volume, or if the 10-year US Treasury yield breaks decisively below 4.20% — the trigger for Singapore bank net interest margin stabilisation.

O39_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. OCBC has reclaimed its 50-day average (SGD 21.34 vs. SGD 20.94, 1.91% above) just two sessions ago on rising averages — the setup is cleaner than DBS but not yet confirmed. Among the three Singapore banks, OCBC was the only one to deliver year-on-year profit growth in Q4 2025 per SGX earnings releases, driven by wealth management fee income that partially offsets the net interest margin compression weighing on the cluster. Trading at 12.0x forward earnings — a 13.7% discount to the Singapore cluster median — and yielding 3.98%, the valuation is modestly attractive, though the 4.44 price-to-earnings-growth ratio signals the market isn't expecting much earnings acceleration. Volume is currently low, which is the one factor keeping this from a clean entry signal; RSI at 56.9 is approaching the threshold where momentum could fade. Entry becomes actionable in the SGD 21.00-21.20 range (near the 50-day average) on a volume pickup, with resistance at SGD 21.81 as the first target. Stop below SGD 20.50.

U11_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to HOLD — UOB has recovered to within 0.69% below its rising 50-day average at SGD 37.33, with RSI stabilising at 52.5, removing the technical deterioration that justified the CAUTION designation. At 10.2x forward earnings — a 26.6% discount to the Singapore cluster median — UOB is the cheapest of the three banks on a multiple basis, but analyst consensus is only HOLD with a forecast 6.3% earnings contraction, and revenue is down 1.8%. The bank faces the same net interest margin compression headwind as its peers, and the one-month return of -3.64% suggests the recent recovery is fragile. No entry dislocation exists from a cash investor's perspective — wait for a confirmed reclaim of the 50-day average on volume before considering entry, with SGD 34.23 as the key longer-term support if the recovery stalls.

C6L_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to WATCH — Singapore Airlines has reclaimed its 50-day average (SGD 6.67 vs. SGD 6.63, 0.58% above) on a rising average, with RSI at 48.6 (neutral-bearish) and a free cash flow yield of 8.22% providing a meaningful valuation floor. The airline faces a genuine structural headwind from the Hormuz crisis (now in day nine): per an AOG Worldwide logistics report, jet fuel prices have nearly doubled since the conflict began, and air cargo rates have risen more than 20% on some Europe-Middle East routes — both directly compressing SIA's operating margins. However, the thesis has not broken: at 17.5x forward earnings (26% premium to Singapore cluster median, justified by its premium network position), 5.31% dividend yield, and enterprise value/EBITDA of just 6.9x, the stock offers genuine value if fuel costs normalise. Entry in the SGD 6.60-6.67 zone (50-day average) makes sense for patient investors; first resistance at SGD 6.78, then SGD 7.20. The Hormuz situation, not technicals, is the swing factor — watch for any de-escalation signal.

Z74_SI.entry_block: BLOCKED: +8.1% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: RSI 64 (>=55).
Z74_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Signal overridden from WATCH to CAUTION — the pipeline's entry block is definitive: Singtel is 8.1% above its 50-day average at SGD 5.17 and RSI of 63.8 exceeds the 55 threshold required for a trend exception, so no entry is supported at current levels regardless of the fundamental story. The five-week, 5.3% one-month rally has pushed the stock to a 25.6x forward price-to-earnings ratio — a striking 84.2% premium to the Singapore cluster median — with revenue growth of just 0.9%, making the valuation mathematically difficult to defend on fundamentals alone. The stock has been above its 50-day average for 32 sessions on a rising average, which otherwise meets the trend duration criterion, but RSI and extension kill the entry. Becomes interesting on a pullback toward SGD 4.78 (50-day average) or the SGD 4.40 support zone; monitor whether the one-month momentum exhausts as it has in each of the prior two rallies.

NVDA.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to CAUTION — NVDA has slipped back below a declining 50-day average ($181.93 vs. $185.25) with RSI at 47.3, and the GTC 2026 keynote failed to sustain the upgrade to WATCH that appeared in Monday's report, as Jensen Huang's $1 trillion AI chip revenue forecast fell flat with investors according to Yahoo Finance Video coverage of Spear Invest's and D.A. Davidson's analyst reaction. The structural thesis remains intact: per Nvidia's Q4 FY2026 earnings call, Q1 FY2027 guidance is $78 billion — implying the Blackwell architecture is shipping faster than consensus expected and pulling forward revenue analysts had modeled for later in the year. At 16.8x forward earnings with a 1.11 price-to-earnings-growth ratio and 73.2% revenue growth, the valuation is genuinely reasonable for the growth rate — a 95.6% analyst earnings growth forecast from 55 sell-side analysts supports patience. Support sits at $177.41 and then $169.81; the 200-day average at $177.83 is the critical structural level to watch. Entry only becomes compelling on a successful test and hold of $177-178 support on above-average volume, with resistance at $184.47 and then $194.67.

AMD.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. AMD remains 8.79% below a declining 50-day average at $215.23 — a deteriorating technical picture, not an oversold bounce opportunity. Per Reuters (March 18), CEO Lisa Su is visiting Samsung's Pyeongtaek chip facility to discuss expanding cooperation beyond memory into foundry manufacturing, which is strategically interesting but not a near-term revenue catalyst. The fundamental case is genuinely compelling: at 18.3x forward earnings with a 0.58 price-to-earnings-growth ratio and 34.1% revenue growth, AMD is the most attractively valued stock in the semis cluster on a growth-adjusted basis, and a reportedly unverified multi-year Meta GPU supply deal (needs SEC filing confirmation before treating as fact) would validate AMD as a credible alternative to Nvidia at hyperscale. However, with the 50-day average declining and RSI at 44, the technicals argue for patience — defined support sits at $158.03 and $149.47, both 20%+ below current levels, suggesting significant downside remains possible. AMD earnings on approximately May 6; monitor the Samsung visit outcome and any foundry partnership formalisation as potential signals of a thesis upgrade.

INTC.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 44x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
INTC.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Intel trades at 44.5x forward earnings — a 143% premium to the semis cluster median — with negative revenue growth (-4.1%) and negative free cash flow yield (-2.05%), making the valuation impossible to justify on current fundamentals alone; the pipeline's soft block on the 44x forward price-to-earnings ratio is appropriate. The thesis is entirely forward-looking: Intel's 18A process node (a chip-making technology that enables advanced 3-dimensional die stacking, per Yahoo Finance's foundry roadmap coverage) represents a potential manufacturing inflection, and High-NA EUV tool installation for the 14A node (per ASML's Q4 2025 earnings call and Intel investor day) extends the roadmap. Intel earnings on approximately April 24 will be the first real test of whether foundry customer wins are materialising on a timeline that justifies the multiple. Key support at $42.58 (about 3.4% below current price); the 200-day average at $33.81 is the structural floor if the foundry thesis disappoints. No entry until either the stock approaches the $42.58 support with improving volume or the April 24 earnings print shows evidence of customer traction for the 18A node.

ASML.signal_rationale: Signal changed from HOLD to WATCH — ASML has reclaimed its 50-day average ($1,389.16 vs. $1,379.50, 0.70% above) for the second consecutive session on a rising average, with RSI at 50.5, converting what was a neutral HOLD into a technically clean watch setup. ASML is the only company in the world that makes the extreme ultraviolet lithography machines required to manufacture the world's most advanced chips, giving it a monopoly-like position that justifies a premium — UBS reiterated a Buy with a €1,500 price target as recently as March 10, per Insider Monkey's coverage. At 32.2x forward earnings with a 2.3 price-to-earnings-growth ratio and only 4.9% revenue growth, the valuation is elevated; the stock trades at a 76% premium to the semis cluster median. However, the High-NA EUV ramp for Intel's 14A node (H2 2026, per ASML's Q4 2025 earnings call) and growing memory chip capex from SK Hynix and Micron provide a multi-quarter demand catalyst. Entry in the $1,379-1,389 range (50-day average zone) with a stop below $1,330; ASML reports earnings approximately April 15 — a key catalyst to monitor. Resistance at $1,407 and $1,491.

MU.entry_block: BLOCKED: +17.2% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=7 (<30), RSI 64 (>=55).
MU.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. Signal overridden from BUY to WATCH — Micron is 17.2% above its 50-day average with only 7 sessions above that average and RSI at 64.2, failing all three criteria required for a trend exception (minimum 30 sessions above average, RSI below 55, and less than 20% extension). Micron reports earnings today (March 18 after close), per the earnings calendar — this is a binary event that makes any new entry position ahead of the print particularly risky. The fundamental setup is genuinely exciting: at 8.1x forward earnings with a 0.63 price-to-earnings-growth ratio, 56.7% revenue growth, and 175.4% analyst earnings growth forecast, Micron is the most fundamentally cheap stock in the semis cluster relative to its growth rate. Micron's CEO has projected roughly 40% memory price increases through Q2 2026 — a bullish call worth noting, though it comes from a seller with an obvious interest in higher prices and needs independent corroboration. The key support to watch post-earnings is $357.67-$363.90; a pullback toward that zone on above-average volume following any sell-the-news reaction would be the cleanest entry opportunity on the watchlist.

AMZN.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to HOLD — Amazon has recovered 8.25% over the past month to $215.20, now only 3.37% below a declining 50-day average at $222.70, with RSI stabilising at 51.4 — no longer technically deteriorating but not yet at an entry-worthy level. Per the Wall Street Journal (March 17), Amazon plans to cut package volume through the US Postal Service by at least two-thirds before its contract expires this fall, reflecting the company's ongoing shift toward in-house logistics infrastructure — a margin-positive move if execution holds. At 23x forward earnings with a 1.94 price-to-earnings-growth ratio and 13.6% revenue growth, Amazon is fairly valued relative to its BigTech peers. The $200 billion capital expenditure commitment (per Q4 2025 earnings call and 10-K) for AI infrastructure is the central tension — massive spend that weighs on near-term free cash flow (yield of just 1.03%) while building long-term AWS competitive position. Upgrades to WATCH on a sustained reclaim of the 50-day average at $222 with volume; support at $211.22 and $196.93.

GOOG.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Alphabet remains 2.92% below a declining 50-day average at $318.71, with RSI at 49.9 — technically weak but not oversold enough to trigger a contrarian entry. The fundamental story is mixed: 18% revenue growth and 31.1% analyst earnings growth look healthy at 23.1x forward earnings (in line with the BigTech cluster median), but a 2.29 price-to-earnings-growth ratio signals the market is already paying for the growth. Google's AI platform expansion — Gemini integration across Search and Chrome per GuruFocus coverage — is strategically significant but not a near-term price catalyst. The stock needs to reclaim $319 (50-day average) on volume before it becomes actionable for a cash investor; resistance then at $328. Support at $295.95. Google reports earnings approximately April 24 — that's the next meaningful binary event that could shift the signal.

LITE.entry_block: BLOCKED: +25.4% above 50-day SMA (>20% extreme extension). No trend exception applies at this level — entry requires a meaningful pullback regardless of trend duration.
LITE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Signal overridden from BUY to CAUTION — Lumentum is 25.45% above its 50-day average, triggering the extreme extension hard block with no exceptions permitted regardless of trend duration or fundamentals. Per the Wall Street Journal and Barron's (March 17), Lumentum fell more than 5% intraday Tuesday after Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang stated the company would use both copper and optical networking — not a shift away from optical, but the market had priced in optical dominance, and the 'both' framing disappointed. Lumentum's S&P 500 inclusion (March 9, per S&P Dow Jones Indices press release) and a reportedly $2 billion Nvidia strategic investment (UNVERIFIED — requires an SEC 8-K filing to confirm before treating as fact) drove the prior surge, but the inclusion-driven momentum appears exhausted per the pattern identified in recent reports. At 44.9x forward earnings but a striking 0.14 price-to-earnings-growth ratio with 65.5% revenue growth, the fundamental case is exceptional — but the technical entry is simply not there. A pullback to the $528 support zone (near the prior consolidation area) would be the earliest point where re-entry becomes discussable; the 50-day average at $517.77 is the ideal entry target.

COHR.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to WATCH — Coherent has pulled back meaningfully (down 5.69% over five days) and now sits 9.31% above its rising 50-day average at $224.87, with RSI at 51.0, which still exceeds the 5% extension threshold for a clean entry but is far more reasonable than LITE's extreme positioning. The same Nvidia GTC copper-versus-optical narrative that hit LITE also pressured COHR, but per the Wall Street Journal's coverage of Huang's actual comments, Nvidia intends to use both technologies — which is actually a confirmation of optical's role in the ecosystem. Coherent has been above its 50-day average for 133 consecutive sessions on a rising average — a well-established trend. At 33.4x forward earnings with a remarkable 0.05 price-to-earnings-growth ratio and 17.5% revenue growth, Coherent trades at a meaningful discount to LITE within the photonics cluster. The pipeline blocks clean entry given the 9.31% extension and RSI above 55; entry becomes actionable on a pullback to the $230-235 zone (approximately 2-3% above the 50-day average with RSI cooling toward 45-50). Resistance at $247.15, then $300.

XLE.entry_block: BLOCKED: +11.6% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: RSI 74 (>=55).
XLE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Signal overridden from HOLD to CAUTION — XLE is 11.61% above its 50-day average with RSI at 73.5 (overbought), triggering a hard pipeline block; the ETF is not approachable at current levels for a cash investor. The Hormuz crisis (now day nine) has driven XLE up 7.65% over the past month and 5.23% over the past five days — energy's performance as a market leader, as Yahoo Finance noted Tuesday, predates the conflict and has been amplified by it. Per CNN reporting citing Lloyd's List Intelligence, only 90 tankers have transited the Strait since March 1 versus a daily baseline of roughly 138, and Iran itself continues exporting oil through the strait via its own tankers. The risk-reward is unfavourable for new entry: RSI overbought reads suggest near-term exhaustion risk, and if WTI demand destruction accelerates (the $90 support remains the key level), XLE could reverse sharply. Avoid at current levels; revisit only if XLE pulls back toward the $52-53 zone (50-day average) or WTI stabilises above $95 with a clear supply reduction signal. ETF-specific valuation metrics are not meaningful here — defer entirely to price action and oil fundamentals.

PLTR.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 83x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
PLTR.signal_rationale: Signal changed from HOLD to WATCH — Palantir has reclaimed its 50-day average ($155.08 vs. $153.10, 1.3% above) for the first session on a flat-to-declining average, with RSI at 57.4 just below the 60 threshold, moving this from a neutral HOLD to a watch setup — though only barely. The defense AI thesis has strengthened: per a Simply Wall St. article citing government contract databases, Palantir and Keel Holdings are deploying Palantir's Foundry and AI Platform (the data integration and AI analytics software used across defence and industrial workflows) across US Navy shipbuilding operations under the ShipOS initiative, with authorised funding reportedly approaching $500 million; separately, an Army IDIQ framework ceiling of $10 billion (an indefinite delivery/indefinite quantity contract — a ceiling amount, not guaranteed revenue) and a $448 million Navy firm contract per a Department of Defense press release support the defense revenue thesis. The 83x forward price-to-earnings ratio is the central obstacle: the pipeline's soft block requires explicit justification. With 70% revenue growth, Rosenblatt raising their price target to $200, and Daiwa upgrading to Buy at $180 per analyst notes cited in catalyst data, the earnings growth trajectory (647.6% analyst forecast) provides that justification — but the 3.22 price-to-earnings-growth ratio and 259% premium to the BigTech cluster median make this a high-conviction-required position. Entry in the $150-153 zone (50-day average) on a volume confirmation; resistance at $187 and $190.

VUAA_L.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Upgrades to WATCH on a sustained reclaim of the 50-day average at $132.24 with above-average volume and VIX below 20.

singapore.summary: The Singapore cluster produced two signal changes today — UOB upgraded from CAUTION to HOLD as it approaches its rising 50-day average, and SIA upgraded from CAUTION to WATCH on a clean reclaim of its average — but the banking trio (DBS, UOB, OCBC) remains constrained by the structural net interest margin compression narrative as long as US10Y holds above 4.20%. The cluster divergence to watch is SIA: unlike the banks, its catalyst path (Hormuz de-escalation reducing jet fuel costs) is independent of Fed policy, making it the most interesting setup in Singapore right now.
singapore.key_development: SIA upgraded to WATCH as it reclaims its 50-day average while sustaining an 8.22% free cash flow yield, offering the cleanest risk-reward in the cluster despite ongoing Hormuz-driven fuel cost inflation.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster is in a state of maximum divergence: ASML upgraded to WATCH on a 50-day average reclaim, Micron is in earnings blackout (reports today) after a 17% extension makes it untouchable technically, NVDA slipped back to CAUTION as GTC's keynote failed to sustain investor enthusiasm, and AMD and Intel remain on CAUTION with their own distinct headwinds. The cluster's fundamental setup is arguably the strongest on the watchlist — low price-to-earnings-growth ratios across AMD (0.58), MU (0.63), and ASML — but technicals and the Fed's rate-pause narrative continue to cap entries.
semiconductors.key_development: Micron earnings today are the single most important cluster event this week — the result will either validate the AI memory demand thesis that underpins the entire semis bull case or expose it as premature.

big_tech.summary: BigTech remains the weakest cluster on the watchlist, with GOOG still on CAUTION below a declining 50-day average, AMZN upgraded to HOLD but not yet actionable, and PLTR upgraded to WATCH on defense AI contract momentum — the latter is pulling the cluster's signal distribution higher. The Hormuz-driven logistics cost story is a secondary headwind for Amazon's margins that the USPS volume reduction partially addresses, but the $200 billion capital expenditure overhang continues to weigh on free cash flow across the cluster.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — defense AI diverging from consumer/ad tech
big_tech.key_development: Palantir's Navy ShipOS deployment and Army IDIQ framework elevate it above the cluster's broader weakness, with the stock reclaiming its 50-day average for the first time in recent sessions.

photonics.summary: The Nvidia GTC 'copper and optical both' message created intraday volatility in LITE and COHR Tuesday but ultimately confirms optical networking's role in the AI infrastructure stack — a neutral-to-positive clarification that was temporarily misread as bearish. COHR's signal upgrade to WATCH reflects its more manageable extension (9.31% vs. LITE's 25.45%) and 133 sessions of trend establishment; LITE remains hard-blocked and requires a meaningful pullback before becoming discussable.
photonics.key_development: Jensen Huang's explicit confirmation that Nvidia will use both copper and optical networking removes the binary risk of optical being displaced — the question is now about optical's share of spend, not its survival.

energy.summary: XLE is overbought (RSI 73.5) and 11.6% above its 50-day average — technically unapproachable despite the Hormuz supply bottleneck remaining unresolved at day nine. The cluster is a single-ticker watchlist position, making the energy thesis entirely dependent on XLE's technical reset before offering a re-entry.
energy.key_development: Per CNN citing Windward data, Iran is exporting nearly the same oil volumes as pre-conflict through a combination of direct Hormuz transits and transponder manipulation — this partial supply offset is the key reason WTI hasn't sustained above $100 despite the strait disruption.

index.summary: VUAA.L remains on CAUTION at 2.11% below a declining 50-day average, functioning as the portfolio's broad market barometer — its signal will improve only when SPY's technical structure repairs and VIX sustains below 20. The tactical relief rally since Monday is not sufficient to change the index outlook.
index.key_development: VIX at 22.37 (down nearly 5% today) continues its compression from last week's 27+ readings, but the index ETF needs a 50-day average reclaim with volume to signal anything beyond tactical relief.

interconnected.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC)
interconnected.reason: Manufactures NVDA's Blackwell chips and AMD's MI-series GPUs — any Micron earnings guidance confirming sustained AI server demand tonight directly supports TSMC's advanced node utilisation rate through H2 2026.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if TSM pulls back 8-10% from current levels, aligning with a broader semis correction that would also test ASML's 50-day average.

interconnected.name: SK Hynix
interconnected.reason: The leading supplier of high-bandwidth memory (the premium memory chips stacked inside AI accelerators) used in NVDA's Blackwell architecture — a positive Micron earnings print tonight for AI memory demand is equally bullish for SK Hynix's order book.
interconnected.entry_note: Monitor after Micron's earnings result today; if MU confirms 40%+ memory price momentum with volume, SK Hynix becomes worth adding to the watchlist on any 10%+ pullback from recent highs.

interconnected.name: Broadcom
interconnected.reason: Supplies custom AI accelerator chips (XPUs) to Alphabet and Meta, making it a direct read-through on hyperscaler AI capex — GOOG's TPU strategy and AMZN's Trainium custom silicon both inform Broadcom's custom silicon pipeline.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if AVGO pulls back toward its 50-day moving average following any broad tech selloff triggered by a hawkish FOMC statement on March 19.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: The dominant supplier of high-speed networking switches for AI data centres — Nvidia's GTC confirmation that both copper and optical networking will be used validates Arista's ethernet switching position as a necessary complement to both approaches.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Arista pulls back 7-10% from current levels or if a broad data centre capex pullback creates an entry near its 50-day average.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Provides custom AI networking chips and data centre interconnect solutions — directly exposed to the same hyperscaler capex cycle as LITE and COHR, with the added benefit of being in the copper networking stack that Nvidia confirmed it will continue using at GTC.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after Micron's earnings tonight; if the AI infrastructure capex thesis is confirmed, Marvell becomes interesting on any pullback of 8-12% from current levels toward its 50-day average.

active_risks: Iran-Hormuz conflict: strait traffic collapsed to ~2 daily vessels vs 138 baseline, threatening ~20% of global oil supply
US-China tech export controls and 100% tariffs reshaping semiconductor supply chains
Fed rate-pause trajectory and sticky inflation constraining tech valuations
geopolitical.macro_outlook: The Hormuz crisis enters its ninth day with no de-escalation signal — per CNN citing Lloyd's List Intelligence, only 90 tankers have transited since March 1, while Iran continues exporting its own oil through the strait using transponder manipulation, partially offsetting the supply shock. The FOMC rate decision tomorrow is the week's most important domestic macro event; the Fed is expected to hold, and any hint of prolonged pause will compress tech multiples further. US-China tariff escalation continues to create a bifurcated semis outlook — domestic US foundry names and memory suppliers benefit from supply chain reshoring narratives, while globally exposed names like ASML face uncertainty around their China revenue normalisation.

base_case.description: Hormuz disruption persists at current reduced traffic levels (2-10 vessels/day) for 4-8 more weeks, keeping WTI in the $90-100 range. FOMC holds rates tomorrow with a data-dependent tone. US-China tariff regime stays at current levels with no new escalation. Markets trade sideways with sector rotation continuing — semis and defense outperform, mega-cap tech underperforms.
base_case.market_impact: SPY holds $655-680 range. Energy names remain elevated but overbought. Semis diverge with mid-caps (MU, AMD) outperforming mega-caps. Singapore banks remain pressured. VIX stays in 18-25 range.
base_case.portfolio_action: Hold current stance across all clusters. No new broad index exposure via VUAA.L. Prioritise ASML and OCBC as the two cleanest watch setups — both are near their 50-day averages with improving technicals. Wait for Micron earnings result tonight before assessing semis signal changes.

optimistic_case.description: Iran-US back-channel negotiations lead to a partial Hormuz reopening or ceasefire announcement within 2 weeks, tanker traffic recovers to 50%+ of baseline. FOMC signals a more accommodative path than expected. WTI drops toward $80-85 on supply restoration.
optimistic_case.market_impact: XLE reverses sharply (-8 to -12% from current levels as the geopolitical premium unwinds). SIA (C6L.SI) rallies on fuel cost normalisation. Singapore banks stabilise. SPY could rally 3-5% on rate optimism plus energy cost relief. Tech multiples expand.
optimistic_case.portfolio_action: If Hormuz reopening signals emerge, SIA becomes an immediate buy at current levels and XLE becomes a sell candidate. Watch for OCBC and DBS to upgrade to WATCH on any US10Y break below 4.20%. Consider adding ASML on the broad tech relief.

pessimistic_case.description: Hormuz disruption escalates with Iranian strikes on Gulf Cooperation Council infrastructure or a US military response that expands the conflict. WTI breaks above $100 sustainably. FOMC turns more hawkish than expected citing energy-driven inflation re-acceleration.
pessimistic_case.market_impact: WTI surges toward $110-120. XLE initially spikes but faces demand destruction reversal if crude exceeds $115. SIA faces severe margin pressure. SPY tests $640-650 support. VIX re-spikes above 30. Singapore banks face additional pressure from flight to safety flows.
pessimistic_case.portfolio_action: Pause all new entries. If SPY breaks below $655, avoid PLTR and photonics names — LITE and COHR face sharpest forced selling from extended positions. SIA (C6L.SI) becomes an avoid despite its valuation support. Hold cash and wait for VIX to exceed 30 as a potential contrarian entry signal for semis.

wildcard.description: US-China trade war escalates beyond current tariff levels — new semiconductor-specific restrictions target ASML's existing DUV machine installed base in China, or China retaliates with rare-earth export controls that constrain NVDA and AMD production timelines.
wildcard.market_impact: ASML drops 10-15% as China revenue (historically elevated share) faces structural impairment. AMD and NVDA face supply chain disruption risk. Rare-earth controls create production cost inflation across semis.
wildcard.portfolio_action: ASML, AMD, and NVDA face sharpest near-term impact — pause all semis entries if this scenario materialises. Singapore names (D05.SI, O39.SI) could see safe-haven inflows as the least exposed cluster to US-China tech friction.

events_this_week.event: Micron Technology (MU) earnings — after market close
FOMC two-day meeting Day 2 (decision tomorrow March 19) — market positioning today ahead of rate decision

FOMC Rate Decision — 14:00 ET. Fed expected to hold; watch Chair Powell's language on inflation trajectory and pace of future cuts. A hawkish hold (emphasising sticky inflation) would pressure tech multiples; a dovish hold (signalling willingness to cut in H1) would be a catalyst for ASML, PLTR, and Singapore bank upgrades.
ASML earnings — first major data point on EUV tool orders and China revenue normalisation; critical for validating the WATCH signal and potential upgrade to BUY.
Intel (INTC) earnings — key test of whether 18A foundry node is attracting customer tapeouts that justify the 44.5x forward price-to-earnings ratio.
Alphabet (GOOG) earnings — advertising revenue trajectory and AI platform monetisation update; potential CAUTION-to-WATCH upgrade catalyst.
DBS Group (D05.SI) earnings — net interest margin trajectory and wealth management fee offset will determine whether the HOLD signal upgrades or deteriorates.

AMD Ryzen AI 400 Series desktop launch — per AMD's MWC 2026 press release, OEM availability from Dell, HP, and Lenovo expected Q2 2026; potential CAUTION-to-WATCH catalyst if paired with technical improvement.
AMD MI450-based GPU shipments to Meta (reportedly, per unverified news reports — needs SEC filing confirmation) — if confirmed, validates AMD as a credible Nvidia alternative at hyperscale scale.
ASML High-NA EUV ramp for Intel 14A node — per ASML Q4 2025 earnings call and Intel investor day; the most important structural driver for ASML's 2026-2027 revenue.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Cautiously defensive with selective technical improvement
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: The FOMC rate decision tomorrow and Micron earnings tonight are the two binary events that could materially shift multiple signals simultaneously — entering new positions today ahead of both events carries elevated binary risk that argues for restraint.

watch_for_entry.note: Closest to entry on the watchlist — OCBC sits 1.91% above its rising 50-day average, the only Singapore bank with YoY profit growth in Q4 2025. Actionable in the SGD 21.00-21.20 zone on a volume pickup.
SIA reclaimed its 50-day average at SGD 6.63 with 8.22% free cash flow yield. Hormuz de-escalation is the swing catalyst — enter at current levels or on any dip to SGD 6.60.

Earnings tonight — do not enter ahead of binary print. If sell-the-news reaction brings MU to $357-364 support zone, that is the entry opportunity. Watch for post-earnings volume signal.

on_deck.note: Best price-to-earnings-growth ratio in the semis cluster at 0.58x with 34.1% revenue growth — fundamentally compelling. Technically broken below declining 50-day average.
on_deck.rationale: 18.3x forward earnings, 0.58 PEG, 34.1% revenue growth, 217% analyst earnings growth forecast. Free cash flow yield thin at 1.43% but earnings ramp supports patience.
on_deck.trigger: 50-day average ($215) flattens and price reclaims it on above-average volume, or Lisa Su's Samsung visit results in a formalised foundry partnership announcement within 30 days.

caution_trim.note: Avoid at current levels — GTC keynote failed to sustain investor enthusiasm, stock slipped back below declining 50-day average. Wait for $177-178 support test.
Avoid — 44.5x forward earnings with negative revenue growth and negative free cash flow yield; thesis is entirely dependent on 18A customer wins not yet confirmed.
Avoid — below declining 50-day average; no entry catalyst before April 24 earnings. Risk-reward unfavourable at current levels.

Avoid — RSI at 73.5 (overbought), 11.6% above 50-day average. Risk-reward unfavourable; wait for pullback to $52-53 zone.
Avoid — 8.1% above 50-day average with RSI 63.8; 84% valuation premium to Singapore cluster with 0.9% revenue growth cannot be justified at current entry levels.
Avoid broad index exposure — below declining 50-day average, structural repair not yet evident. Wait for VIX below 20 and 50-day average reclaim.

hold_no_action.note: Hugging its 50-day average — no entry dislocation, no deterioration. Upgrades to WATCH on pullback to SGD 57.00 or US10Y break below 4.20%.
Upgraded from CAUTION to HOLD — approaching rising 50-day average. No action until confirmed reclaim with volume. Reassess below SGD 34.23.
Upgraded from CAUTION to HOLD — recovering but still below declining 50-day average at $222.70. Upgrades to WATCH on sustained reclaim of that level with volume.

contrarian_candidates.watching_for: A test and hold of the $191-196 zone (200-day average to current price) on above-average volume, combined with confirmation from Micron earnings tonight that AI chip demand remains robust — that combination would shift AMD toward a WATCH signal.

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Revenue beat with memory price guidance of 30-40% increase confirmed; HBM demand for AI accelerators cited as a multi-quarter tailwind.
bullish_upgrades: MU — pullback to $357-364 support becomes a BUY entry
AMD — on-deck status strengthens; moves closer to WATCH upgrade
NVDA — AI demand confirmation supports thesis; watch for $177-178 support hold
ASML — HBM capex confirmation supports EUV tool order backlog thesis
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Revenue miss or guidance cut; memory price recovery described as slower than expected or demand from AI data centres plateauing.
bearish_impact: MU — remains blocked; $363 support becomes a test level to watch for failure
AMD — further pressure on CAUTION; on-deck designation at risk of removal
NVDA — additional selling pressure on declining 50-day average; $169.81 becomes the key support
ASML — watch signal at risk if HBM capex cycle narrative weakens

AMZN — reclaims declining 50-day average; HOLD upgrades to WATCH
GOOG — 50-day average reclaim becomes more likely; CAUTION upgrades to WATCH

PLTR — 83x forward earnings becomes increasingly difficult to justify; WATCH reverts to HOLD
